title: Aneurolepidium angustum
intro: Aneurolepidium angustum, also known as Eulalia or Wild Celery, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating wounds and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Aneurolepidium angustum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the form of tinctures, extracts, and powders to harness its health benefits.
